title: July 2022 — 'don't want something you're not willing to work for'
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-only July 2022 maxim: "Don't want something that you are not willing to work for."
  • The hustle-pole counterweight to two days earlier's flow-frame — Yahir is openly oscillating between wu wei and max effort commitment in the same week.
  • A tighter restatement of the max effort commit principle that becomes a recurring mantra through 2023–25.
  • Sits in the summer 2022 philosophy mini-cluster with 2022 06 10 always give take comes and 2022 07 22 relax let thing flow — three short maxims in 6 weeks, each a different posture.
  • Aphorism-grade enough to be quoted; reads like internalized Connor McGregor / Shaan Puri tape.

What's actually in the note

(Body empty — entire content is the title)

Don't want something that you are not willing to work for.

Why it matters

Two graph-useful observations:

  1. Want-as-contract. Reframes desire as a check-yourself filter: if you're not willing to put in the work, you don't actually want it. This is the max effort commit frame in seed form.
  2. Two-pole 2022 voice. Read against 2022 07 22 relax let thing flow (trust the flow) and this (don't want what you won't earn), the 2022 Yahir is not coherent — he's iterating. The summer 2022 philosophy cluster is the data on that iteration.

Open questions

  • What was he wanting at the time? (Likely the osig outcome, possibly a relationship — see 2022 09 04 never been in love vietnam for the romance frame; likely also early mom house by the river goal thinking.)
  • Did the maxim hold? (His 2022 09 23 buy mom a house note three months later suggests yes — he was scaling commitment to match scale of want.)

Wikilinks
